name: Roast Beef with Gravy (Microwave Method)
recipeIngredient: 1 can beef broth (or use potato water)
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a round, tender, and regularly shaped roast that weighs 3 pounds or more.
2. Place the roast on a microwave roasting rack with the fat side down. Brush with Kitchen Bouquet if desired.
3. Microwave on High for 8 minutes if the roast weighs 3 pounds or less, or for 10 minutes if it weighs more than 3 pounds.
4. Remove the roast from the oven and turn it fat side up. Insert a microwave meat thermometer.
5. Cook on 40% power (low to medium-low) until the thermometer reaches the desired temperature: Rare - 120°F, Medium Rare - 125°F, Medium - 135°F, Well-done - 150°F.
6. Remove the roast from the oven and place it on a plate. Wrap tightly in aluminum foil.
7. Allow the roast to stand for 15 minutes (important - meat will continue to cook during standing time; do not omit this step).
8. To make gravy, pour meat juices from the roasting rack into a saucepan. Rinse the roasting rack with broth or potato water and scrape to remove browned bits.
9. Add broth or potato water until the liquid in the saucepan equals 2 cups. Bring to a boil.
10. Thicken the gravy by stirring a mixture of cornstarch and cold water. Season to taste.
11. Package leftovers according to directions for French Dip Sandwiches.
12. Serving ideas: Mashed Potatoes, Green Beans, Buttermilk Bread
13. Notes: Use leftovers for French Dip Sandwiches.
